List 2-3 key technologies you're proficient in — this immediately positions you as someone with demonstrable, relevant expertise.
Your response is quite short. A complete self-introduction should be 60-90 seconds (roughly 100-180 words). Expand on your background, skills, and goals.
Your speech has noticeable hesitations and 0 long pauses. Practice your introduction until you can deliver it without extended breaks.

records.transcript: Hi, I'm Shambhavish Vastav, currently studying B. Tech, CSE, AIML. That's it.

improvements.text: Sharing a specific project or role you've held demonstrates that you can apply your skills in practice — this is a critical gap in your current introduction.
Your response is quite short. A complete self-introduction should be 60-90 seconds (roughly 100-180 words). Expand on your background, skills, and goals.
Your speech has noticeable hesitations and 0 long pauses. Practice your introduction until you can deliver it without extended breaks.
Your voice energy drops towards the end of your introduction. Your closing is as important as your opening — maintain strong energy throughout, especially in your final statement.
Your English is solid. To reach the next level, practice using more professional vocabulary and complex sentence structures in your introduction.

records.transcript: Hello everyone, my name is Rani Gupta. Today I want to share one thing that really we have seen Rajat sir mood swings today. Rani Gupta.

improvements.text: Adding your educational details (university, degree, year) gives the interviewer context about your training and specialization.
Without mentioning any work experience or projects, your introduction lacks practical evidence of your abilities. Include at least one concrete example.
Naming specific tools, languages, or frameworks you know gives the interviewer concrete evidence of your technical capabilities.
Your response is quite short. A complete self-introduction should be 60-90 seconds (roughly 100-180 words). Expand on your background, skills, and goals.
Your speech has noticeable hesitations and 0 long pauses. Practice your introduction until you can deliver it without extended breaks.
Your voice energy drops towards the end of your introduction. Your closing is as important as your opening — maintain strong energy throughout, especially in your final statement.

records.transcript: Good afternoon, my name is Shawn I am currently a first -year BTEC student in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning at Chandigarh University. I have developed skills in communication, teamwork, and basic programming, along with strong problem -solving abilities. Through my academic exposure and participation in activities, I am gaining experience in working collaboratively and adapting in new challenges. My strengths include being disciplined, a quick learner, and highly adaptable. And I am known for maintaining a positive and confident attitude. I have a keen interest in AI, technology, and continuous learning. My career goal is to build a strong foundation in AI and contribute to innovative solutions in the tech industry. I believe I can add value through my dedication, consistency, and willingness to learn. I look forward to growing, contributing in many ways. Thank you.

improvements.text: Try expanding more on your technical depth and unique experiences to further captivate the interviewer.
Try adding a memorable closing statement that summarizes why you're the ideal candidate.
Practice this introduction 5-10 times until it feels natural and effortless.

records.transcript: Good, good morning. Uh, my name is, uh, Uh, I'm currently pursuing BTech with, uh, AIML, uh, in Chandigarh University. Um, I'm looking forward to opportunities. I have good communication skills. I love to work in I'm a quick learner as well as, uh, I always stay positive while facing new, uh, challenges. I have keen interest for artificial intelligence and, how the world is building. Well, I am eager to learn and grow through new opportunities and, uh, environment. Looking forward to it. Thank you.

improvements.text: Sharing a specific project or role you've held demonstrates that you can apply your skills in practice — this is a critical gap in your current introduction.
Your pronunciation could be clearer. Focus on enunciating each syllable — especially consonants — and practice tongue twisters to improve articulation.
You used 11 filler words (uh). Replace these with confident pauses — silence between sentences sounds much more professional than 'um' or 'uh'.
Your English is solid. To reach the next level, practice using more professional vocabulary and complex sentence structures in your introduction.
